texto: I have experienced bad dating and ineptitude with women all across the globe, from Vietnam to Paris. When I was 21, women were an enigma; they were this code that had to be cracked. They were 'The Other.' I have often thought writing this stuff into stand-up and shows would be an exorcism, but it hasn't been; it makes no difference.
explicacao: Citação de Stephen Merchant sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “I have experienced bad dating and ineptitude with women all across the globe, from Vietnam to Par…” articula reflexao, women, writing em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como writing e thought.

frase_original: I have experienced bad dating and ineptitude with women all across the globe, from Vietnam to Paris. When I was 21, women were an enigma; they were this code that had to be cracked. They were 'The Other.' I have often thought writing this stuff into stand-up and shows would be an exorcism, but it hasn't been; it makes no difference.

texto: The reason I keep talking about a wife and saying the word 'wife' on stage is because it seems a funny word to me. The more you say it, the more it seems to detach from that person and become this sort of abstract thing: that you would set out to find a wife, that it would be an objective like buying a new car.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Stephen Merchant: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a humor. O trecho “The reason I keep talking about a wife and saying the word 'wife' on stage is because it seems a …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que humor, funny, wife pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de wife e me.
